steps.title: Identify the stated goal
steps.content: The task is to emphasize the order in which the two students attended the Bauhaus. According to the notes, Engelmann began in 1920 and Adler began in 1930, so Engelmann came first. The correct choice must explicitly show that chronological sequence — not just list both students or focus on unrelated details like directors.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Founded in 1919, the Bauhaus was a German art and design school whose past students include Marta Engelmann and Lotte Adler.: Lists both students but states no order between them; neither is said to come before the other. ✗
  • Choice B — Walter Gropius founded the Bauhaus in 1919 and acted as its director until 1928, at which point Hannes Meyer succeeded him in the role.: Focuses entirely on directors; neither student is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — Marta Engelmann and Lotte Adler were both Bauhaus students, but Engelmann, who began attending in 1920, preceded Adler.: Uses the word "preceded" to make the chronological order unmistakable: Engelmann before Adler. ✓
  • Choice D — The Bauhaus was founded in 1919, and Lotte Adler began attending in 1930.: Mentions only Adler; Engelmann is omitted entirely, so no order between the two students can be shown. ✗
C is correct.
